surface.name: ggshield local repository secret scan
surface.selection_basis: Under the workshop policy, the local ggshield install-script route is primary because the official guide calls it the recommended quickest setup, combines installation and browser authentication, and immediately scans a repository. Account creation is one opaque step at the official signup URL.

alternatives_considered.name: GitGuardian platform repository audit
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This path requires connecting repositories and measures historical repository health, while the selected ggshield guide explicitly presents a local first scan.

alternatives_considered.name: Manual ggshield installation and token authentication
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The same official guide labels the install script and browser login as the recommended route.

entry_point.developer_intent: Install ggshield, obtain GitGuardian access, and scan a local Git repository for hardcoded secrets until the CLI reports a completed scan.
assumed_prior_state: A Linux or macOS developer has Git, a shell, and a local Git repository to scan.
No GitGuardian account, workspace, API token, ggshield installation, authentication, or prior scan is assumed.
No account creation, browser authentication, token issuance, or repository scan was performed during research.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Scan your first content with ggshield.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: ggshield has authenticated to a GitGuardian workspace and completed a secret scan of the selected local repository.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The repository scan command exits with code 0 when it completes without detected issues, or code 1 when it completes and detects issues.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Scan your first content with ggshield.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The Getting started page explicitly labels this as the first content scan, and the CLI reference defines its completed-scan exit codes.

prerequisites.requirement: Use Linux or macOS with Git and a shell for the selected install-script route; the guide also lists Windows and manual installation alternatives.
Have a local Git repository available at a known path.
Have or create a GitGuardian account and workspace.
Authenticate ggshield with an API token provisioned through browser login or supplied manually.

primary_path.action: Open the ggshield Getting started page and select the recommended Linux or macOS install-script route.
primary_path.output: Requirements, installation, authentication, and first-scan sections are visible.
primary_path.success_signal: The page identifies the install script as the recommended quickest setup.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A different operating system requires another documented installation branch.

primary_path.input: A supported shell with network access.
primary_path.output: ggshield is installed and the script invokes `ggshield auth login`.
primary_path.success_signal: The CLI begins the browser authentication route.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Installation or network errors stop setup; the guide supplies manual alternatives.

primary_path.action: Open GitGuardian browser authentication and instruct the developer to log in or create an account.
primary_path.input: The login request started by ggshield.
primary_path.output: A browser login or signup surface is opened.
primary_path.success_signal: The browser offers an account-access route.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A valid GitGuardian account and workspace are required before a token can be provisioned.

primary_path.action: Sign up for or sign in to GitGuardian and select a workspace.
details: Treat signup as one opaque workshop-policy step at the official signup URL.
primary_path.success_signal: The browser authentication flow can authorize ggshield.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Follow the displayed account or workspace recovery path.

details: The automatic login provisions a personal access token and stores it in ggshield's configuration.
primary_path.input: Authenticated workspace and pending `ggshield auth login` request
primary_path.success_signal: The browser flow completes and returns control to an authenticated CLI.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Run `ggshield auth login` again or use the documented manual token route.

primary_path.input: Authenticated ggshield and local Git repository
primary_path.success_signal: The command exits 0 when no issues are detected or exits 1 when issues are detected; both indicate a completed scan.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Authentication and usage errors use other documented exit codes.

branches.condition: Do not use the install script
branches.path: Use a documented standalone binary, package manager, pip, pipx, or Docker installation, then run `ggshield auth login` manually.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes installation mechanics but still converges on required GitGuardian authentication.

branches.path: Click Sign up with GitHub and follow GitHub instructions to install the GitGuardian GitHub App for all or selected repositories.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Introduces external GitHub authorization and repository-scope choices before account convergence.

branches.path: Complete the signup form, receive GitGuardian email, and follow its instructions.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Introduces an email verification or continuation wait whose detailed screens are not documented.

branches.condition: An organization already uses GitGuardian SSO
branches.path: Enter the professional identity and follow the organization workspace SSO redirect.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Replaces individual signup with organization-controlled access and permissions.

friction_gates.description: The selected route downloads and executes GitGuardian's published installer before authentication.
All ggshield scans use the GitGuardian API and require a workspace plus API token.
A new user must choose GitHub, email, or organization SSO account access.
The GitHub route requires GitGuardian App installation and a choice of all or selected repositories.
The email route requires receiving and following a GitGuardian email.

sources.title: Getting started | GitGuardian documentation
sections_used: Scan your first content with ggshield

sources.title: Create your GitGuardian account | GitGuardian documentation
GitGuardian
evidence_supported: Signup page requires client-side JavaScript and exposes no reproducible form steps in the public response

sources.title: ggshield secret scan repo | GitGuardian documentation

excluded_after_success: Pre-commit hooks, CI integration, incident remediation, repository monitoring, policy tuning, and historical platform audits occur after the first local repository scan.
